text.title: React Architecture Antipatterns
text.summary: Prop drilling, giant components, business logic in JSX, default exports, deep imports, and use client overuse.

antipattern.description: Prop drilling: passing the same prop through 3+ component levels.
antipattern.reason: Deep prop chains couple the intermediate components to data they do not use. Any change to the prop shape requires updates at every level. Use React context or move data to a feature hook.

antipattern.description: Giant component: a single component file exceeding 300 lines mixing rendering, data fetching, and business logic.
antipattern.reason: Mega-components cannot be unit-tested in isolation, are hard to review, and accumulate future tech debt. The 250-line cap is the forcing function to split early.

antipattern.description: Business logic (validation, API calls, error handling) placed directly inside component functions instead of custom hooks or services.
antipattern.reason: Business logic in JSX cannot be tested without rendering. Extracting to hooks enables unit tests without a DOM, and makes the component's rendering responsibility clear.
Default exports produce ambiguous names on import, break rename-symbol refactoring tools, and prevent barrel files from being reliably tree-shaken.

antipattern.description: LLM adds 'use client' defensively to every component.
antipattern.reason: Marking a component as client when it has no client-side interactivity prevents RSC optimization and increases bundle size. Audit each generated component for actual client-side dependencies before accepting the directive.

antipattern.description: Agent imports from deep paths (src/components/ui/Button/Button) instead of the barrel (src/components/ui/Button).
antipattern.reason: Deep imports couple consumers to the internal file structure. When the internal layout changes, all deep imports break. Enforce barrel imports with an ESLint rule against deep imports inside components/ui/.

antipattern.description: Agent adds a new compound subcomponent (Card.Action) but forgets to add the named export to index.ts.
antipattern.reason: The component is accessible via the namespace alias but invisible to the barrel, causing missing exports and tree-shaking failures. Review every compound component addition for index.ts coverage.
